old_claim.note: Fiber Optics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Flexible fiber-optic endoscopy was the clinical optical platform that preceded and enabled electronic video endoscopy.

source_shape.source_locator: Dittrick Medical History Center artifact page for the Hirschowitz fiberoptic endoscope.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The museum page describes practical fiberoptic endoscopy beginning in 1957 and the 1960 Hirschowitz fiberoptic endoscope artifact.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports the edge as an evidence upgrade because video endoscopy inherited the flexible clinical optical platform before adding electronic image sensors.

ontology_before: The fiber-optics edge was an unsourced generic enabling claim.
ontology_after: Fiber optics remains an enabling predecessor platform for video endoscopy with source-checked historical support.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: fiber optics remains an enabling endoscopy predecessor.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to capsule cameras that do not inherit flexible endoscope optics.
A source showed video endoscopy developed independently of prior fiberoptic endoscopy practice.
The edge were upgraded to required and confused predecessor platform with the electronic video component.
short_reason: Fiberoptic endoscopy is source-backed predecessor context for video endoscopy.
